PhoenixmlDb

Performance Tuning

Query optimization, storage tuning, memory management, and monitoring

#Performance Tuning

Optimize PhoenixmlDb for your specific workload with these tuning guidelines.

#Query Optimization

#Use Indexes

The most important optimization is proper indexing:

#Check Query Plans

#Optimize XQuery

#Limit Results

#Use Parameters

#Storage Optimization

#Appropriate Map Size

#Sync Mode

#Batch Writes

#Memory Management

#Read-Only Transactions

#Dispose Promptly

#Stream Large Documents

#Index Tuning

#Index Selectivity

High-selectivity indexes are more effective:

#Composite Indexes

#Index Maintenance

#Monitoring

#Database Statistics

#Query Metrics

#Benchmarking

#Measure Operations

#Compare Approaches

#Common Bottlenecks

Symptom

Likely Cause

Solution

Slow queries

Missing index

Add appropriate index

High memory

Large transactions

Use smaller batches

Slow writes

Sync overhead

Use NoMetaSync or batch

Lock contention

Long transactions

Keep transactions short

"Map full"

Insufficient size

Increase MapSize

#Platform Tuning

#Linux

#Windows

#Best Practices Summary

  1. Index first — Create indexes for query patterns

  2. Batch writes — Group writes in transactions

  3. Use read-only — When only reading

  4. Check plans — Verify index usage

  5. Monitor metrics — Track performance over time

  6. Keep transactions short — Minimize lock duration

  7. Stream large data — Avoid loading into memory

  8. SSD storage — For production workloads
